title: Protocol: vaccine clinical trial concentration across Africa
primary_estimand: Share of African vaccine trials located in South Africa
body: This cross-sectional registry study will describe how interventional vaccine trial activity is distributed across twelve African countries using ClinicalTrials.gov data. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for vaccine trials by country, sponsor, phase, and vaccine type using filter.advanced and query.cond for vaccine conditions, comparing African totals with those of the United States and United Kingdom as high-income benchmarks. The primary outcome is the share of all African vaccine trials located in South Africa, quantifying geographic concentration of vaccine research on the continent. Secondary analyses will examine country-level distribution, vaccine type breakdown across eight categories including malaria, HIV, COVID, rotavirus, pneumococcal, meningococcal, Ebola, and tuberculosis vaccines, sponsor classification distinguishing pharmaceutical from public funders, and phase mix to distinguish late-stage testing from earlier development. Query logic and category definitions will be prespecified and archived. No patient-level data will be used. Limitations include registry dependence and the inability to infer manufacturing capacity or post-trial access from registry records alone.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.name: methods described
checks.detail: Protocol describes API query strategy, filter syntax, stratification, and classification scheme.

checks.name: outcomes specified
checks.detail: Primary (SA South Africa share) and secondary (country, vaccine type, sponsor, phase, relevance, access) outcomes specified.
